UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information, primarily to link user-specific data to their external authentication account (e.g., Firebase Authentication).

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

externalAuthId.description: The unique identifier provided by the external authentication system (e.g., Firebase Authentication User ID) for this user. (Relationship: External Auth 1:1 UserProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

ScamSignature.title: ScamSignature
ScamSignature.description: Stores characteristics and patterns used to identify known scams or deepfake techniques.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ScamSignature entity.

name.description: A human-readable name or title for the specific scam signature.

signatureType.description: The type of media or context this signature applies to (e.g., 'audio', 'video', 'metadata', 'text').

patternDescription.description: A detailed description of the pattern, characteristics, or model ID that this signature represents for detection.

severity.description: The perceived severity level of a scam identified by this signature (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High', 'Critical').

isActive.description: Indicates whether this scam signature is currently active and being used for detection.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the scam signature was added to the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the scam signature was last updated.

DeepfakeDetectionEvent.title: DeepfakeDetectionEvent
DeepfakeDetectionEvent.description: Records instances where media was analyzed for deepfake characteristics and the results of that analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DeepfakeDetectionEvent entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who was involved in or affected by this detection event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N DeepfakeDetectionEvent)

detectionTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the deepfake detection analysis was performed.

sourceMediaType.description: The type of media that was analyzed (e.g., 'audio', 'video', 'mixed').

probabilityScore.description: The calculated probability score (e.g., 0.0 to 1.0) indicating the likelihood of the media being a deepfake.

isDeepfakeDetected.description: Boolean flag indicating whether a deepfake was confirmed based on the probability score exceeding a predefined threshold.

analysisDetails.description: Additional technical details or metadata from the deepfake analysis (e.g., specific features flagged, model version used, raw output).

matchedScamSignatureIds.description: References to ScamSignature entities that were matched during this deepfake detection event. (Relationship: ScamSignature N:N DeepfakeDetectionEvent)

isAlertTriggered.description: Indicates whether an in-app alert was successfully triggered for this detection event.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this deepfake detection event record was created.

UserReport.title: UserReport
UserReport.description: Stores reports submitted by users regarding suspicious activities, deepfake encounters, or other security concerns.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserReport entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who submitted this report. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserReport)

reportType.description: The category or type of incident being reported (e.g., 'Deepfake Encounter', 'Scam Attempt', 'Suspicious Call').

description.description: The user's free-form description of the reported incident.

incidentTimestamp.description: The timestamp when the reported incident occurred, as provided by the user.

deepfakeDetectionEventId.description: Optional reference to a DeepfakeDetectionEvent if this report is directly related to a system-detected deepfake. (Relationship: DeepfakeDetectionEvent 1:N UserReport)

status.description: The current processing status of the report (e.g., 'Pending', 'Under Review', 'Resolved', 'Archived').

attachmentUris.description: An array of URIs to external storage locations for any attachments (e.g., screenshots, recordings) provided by the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user report was submitted.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user report was last updated (e.g., status change).

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. The `userId` wildcard corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID. Access is restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Contains globally accessible characteristics and patterns for identifying scams and deepfakes. These documents are read-only for general users; creation and updates are managed by backend services or administrators.

params.name: signatureId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific scam signature.

definition.description: Records instances of deepfake detection analysis associated with a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches both the path wildcard and the document's 'userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this detection event, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific deepfake detection event.

definition.description: Stores reports submitted by users regarding suspicious activities or deepfake encounters. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches both the path wildcard and the document's 'userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who submitted this report, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user report.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed around core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Mandate A):**
This is achieved by storing the `userId` directly within `DeepfakeDetectionEvent` and `UserReport` documents, even though they are nested under `/userProfiles/{userId}`. For example, a `DeepfakeDetectionEvent` document at `/userProfiles/{someUserId}/deepfakeDetectionEvents/{eventId}` will explicitly contain `"userId": "someUserId"`. This denormalization means that security rules for these subcollections do not need to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `UserProfile` document to determine ownership. Instead, rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path and with the `resource.data.userId` field in the document itself. This eliminates costly and complex `get()` calls in rules, making operations atomic and highly debuggable.

**Structural Segregation (Mandate B):**
Documents are segregated into collections based on their security posture. `ScamSignature` documents, intended for global read access by all users (for detection processes), reside in a top-level collection `/scamSignatures`. In contrast, `UserProfile` and its associated `DeepfakeDetectionEvent` and `UserReport` records, which are private and user-specific, are organized hierarchically under `/userProfiles/{userId}`. This ensures that all documents within a given collection or subcollection share the same access requirements, simplifying rule logic significantly.

**Access Modeling (Mandate C) and QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Private Data:** `UserProfile` documents are stored at `/userProfiles/{userId}`, making `userId` the explicit owner, aligning with Path-Based ownership. Access is strictly `request.auth.uid == userId`.
2.  **User-Owned Hierarchical Data:** `DeepfakeDetectionEvent` and `UserReport` entities are nested under their respective `UserProfile` (`/userProfiles/{userId}/deepfakeDetectionEvents/{eventId}` and `/userProfiles/{userId}/userReports/{reportId}`). This hierarchical path intrinsically links the data to its owner. For `list` operations (Query Against Paths - QAPs), a user can only query their own subcollection (e.g., `/userProfiles/myUserId/deepfakeDetectionEvents`). The security rules can then easily enforce `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path) AND `resource.data.userId == userId` (from the denormalized field), guaranteeing that a user can only list their own events/reports. This prevents unauthorized access and ensures that rules are not used as filters after data retrieval.
3.  **Global Roles (DBAC):** Not directly implemented in entities, but `ScamSignature` is designed for global read access (backend-controlled write), which is a form of global access. Any specific admin roles would reside in dedicated collections (e.g., `/roles_admin/{uid}`) if needed, relying solely on `request.auth.uid` for checks, not custom claims.

**Data Clarity and Predictability (Mandate D):**
*   Descriptive wildcards like `{userId}`, `{signatureId}`, `{eventId}`, `{reportId}` are used for clarity.
*   The `status` field in `UserReport` explicitly models the report's lifecycle, adhering to explicit state modeling. All authorization fields are standardized (e.g., `userId` for ownership).

This robust structure directly addresses all core design principles and mandates, leading to a secure, performant, and maintainable Firestore database for Vishwaas Guard.
